message: We were never just classmates. Thank you for four years of borrowed notes, shared lunches, and the kind of laughter that made the guards check on us. I love you all.
To the loudest section in the building — sorry to every teacher, and thank you to every one of you. See you at the reunion, first round of milk tea is on me.
You held my hand through every quiz I thought would end me. There's no version of these four years without you in it. Best seatmate, best friend, forever.
We never won the championship but we won every halftime. Proud of every bruise. Once a teammate, always a brother.
You believed I could write before I did. Every page of this yearbook exists a little bit because of you. Thank you for everything.
Same spot, same chaos, every single day. If I could enroll again just for lunch period, I would. Keep my seat warm.
We started as strangers in face masks and ended as a family. Whatever happens next, we already did the hard part together.
Hey. It's us, from the last week of high school. Hope you kept in touch. Hope you're happy. Hope you still remember the dance steps. — Z
